mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when the unemployment rate crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in real personal spending and rising consumer prices indicates strong aggregate demand outpacing supply, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects weakening manufacturing output and supply-side contraction, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >65% of months following an industrial production decline during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or payroll growth fails to predict positive returns in >50% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate profits and forces monetary tightening expectations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in >55% of months when inflation crosses the 3.0% threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts alongside expanding real GDP indicate synchronized strength in residential investment and broad economic activity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of quarters, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in industrial production signal robust manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of months following positive industrial production growth over a 5-year walk-forward test.
